ground_truth.title: 5 Trends from Nutrition 2025
ground_truth.author: Dylan Bailey, MS, RD, FAND
ground_truth.main_content:
The Nutrition 2025 meeting hosted annually by the American Society for Nutrition brought together leading experts, thought leaders, and clinicians to Orlando to showcase the latest in nutrition science and practice. I've compiled 5 trends that stood out while walking the expo hall and sitting in sessions over 4 days.

1. From Lab to Literature, Artificial Intelligence Has Strong Showing
Artificial intelligence made a strong showing at Nutrition 2025 as researchers demonstrated its growing role in nutrition science. AI is reshaping research and publishing, with experts sharing how they use AI tools to accelerate and improve data analysis and literature reviews. Beyond scientific publishing, AI is also driving discovery of novel nutrients and bioactive compounds.

2. Science StorEATelling
This year's conference underscored the value of culinary experiences in bringing nutrition science to life. Some lunchtime programs invited attendees to immerse themselves in the science through food. Such interactive learning experiences allowed participants not just to hear about nutrition evidence, but to taste and see it in action.

3. GLP-1 Weight-Loss Drugs Steal the Spotlight With Nutrition Alongside
GLP-1 agonist drugs were everywhere at Nutrition 2025. Researchers presented cutting-edge findings on how these drugs modulate appetite-regulating circuits and metabolic pathways. But a clear theme was that medication alone isn't a magic bullet. Experts emphasized the need for comprehensive strategies combining pharmacology with lifestyle changes.

4. The Ultra-Processed Foods Debate Isn't Going Anywhere
Sessions on ultra-processed foods were abundant at Nutrition 2025. Researchers presented new data from the RESTRUCTURE trial, probing how the sensory properties of foods influence calorie intake and satiety. Consensus was that UPFs will remain a central research and communication topic.

5. Protein Science Continues to Dominate
If one nutrient reigned supreme at Nutrition 2025, it was protein. From muscle health to metabolic advantages, protein was at the center of many presentations. A key focus was on protein quality—not just quantity—and how better metrics like DIAAS can inform global nutrition strategies.
